items.title: BHP workers to strike as negotiations with mining giant stall

children.content: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

children.content: The WA Police commissioner writes to Meta to ask for assistance with Facebook moderation after a number of derogatory comments are left on social media pages.

image.altText: A medium close-up shot of WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch in uniform.

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

children.content: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

image.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: Millions of tiny engineers help to restore health of marine ecosystem in WA

children.content: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

items.title: Foreign investor snub of divestment order 'extraordinary', says expert

children.content: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

image.altText: A remote mining camp in the outback

items.title: Principal warns school could be forced to close after bus assistance cut

children.content: The head of an independent school in regional WA says changes to student travel subsidies could ultimately lead to the institution's demise.

image.altText: An orange school bus drives on a gravel road in Western Australia.

items.title: 'Completely unimaginable': Man critically injured in robot vacuum fire

children.content: The family of a Perth man critically injured when a robot vacuum caught fire at his home say their lives have been "turned upside down" by the "horrific" incident.

items.title: Broken timers trigger nationwide Telstra outage

children.content: Telstra says Wednesday's nationwide outage was caused by malfunctioning "nodes" that manage time synchronisation within some of the network data centres, but has no idea what caused the timer issues.

image.altText: A close up of a Telstra logo on the side of a pay phone

items.title: Critical drugs could be pulled from PBS amid pricing stoush

children.content: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

image.altText: A young woman with pink hair,  wearing a brown knitted cap and pink striped pyjamas, smiles as she takes a selfie.

items.title: More than 120 NSW crown prosecutors take rare step over DPP report

children.content: Senior crown prosecutor Brett Hatfield SC issued the extraordinary statement on behalf of the more than 120 barristers who take on cases for the crown.

image.altText: a woman in legal dress leaving a court

items.title: Discounts 'too little, too late' after slow start to WA's northern tourist season

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

image.altText: A girl stands holding a suitcase at the end of the groyne at Town Beach in Broome looking out to the ocean

items.title: The 'amazing' turnaround in literacy at this primary school

children.content: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school just outside Bunbury in south-west WA could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

children.content: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

children.content: The Reserve Bank's Sarah Hunter says global supply shocks appear to be occurring more frequently and the RBA will try to adjust to the new reality.

children.content: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

items.title: IS-linked Australian accused of abusing Yazidi girl enslaved in her home

children.content: The last of the known Islamic State-linked Australian women in Syria is expected to return home amid allegations a Yazidi girl was enslaved, beaten and raped in the woman's home a decade ago, the ABC can reveal.

image.altText: A group of three women wearing burqas walk along a camp in Syria.

items.title: Court told former MP knew lying was 'part and parcel' of visa scheme

children.content: A court hears former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire knew being dishonest to the Department of Immigration was a component of an alleged fraudulent visa scheme.

items.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

items.title: 'We spoke for Palestine': Egypt fans on historic World Cup after heartbreaking loss

children.content: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

articles.title: Quick hits: Belgium trolls Trump as Egypt fires up over 'fixed' World Cup

articles.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight

items.title: Labor in 'fight for our lives' as by-election opens door to One Nation assault

children.content: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

items.title: Housing inequity in Australia 'must focus beyond age'

items.title: Does Alcoa's $8b acquisition leave WA's jarrah forests in the firing line?
